mission: Create the evidence-processing vendor-selection dossier for transcription and artifact scanning while freezing the internal mock-now lane, the current official vendor evidence set, and the fail-closed actual-provider-later shortlist used by seq_035.

phase0_gate_posture.reason: Planning and architecture foundation are frozen enough to open external-readiness work, but actual Phase 0 entry remains withheld because the current-baseline external-readiness gate is still blocked by onboarding, assurance, and simulator-freeze dependencies.

integration_context.integration_name: Telephony capture, transcript, and artifact-safety backplane

recommended_strategy.label: Split providers, keep local scan bias available
recommended_strategy.summary: Use a focused transcription provider and a separate artifact-scanning provider later. Keep the canonical mock lane internal, reject any automatic single-suite winner, and prefer self-hosted or local scanning when quarantine and residency constraints outweigh procurement simplicity.

lane_matrix.mock_lane_summary: Keep transcript readiness, coverage classes, quality bands, and supersession semantics internal first.
lane_matrix.actual_lane_summary: Admit one focused STT provider later, but only behind callback-hint plus trusted re-fetch rules.
lane_matrix.seq035_handoff_focus: project or account creation, API keys, callback endpoint prep, region choice, and retention deletion posture

lane_matrix.mock_lane_summary: Keep artifact quarantine manifests, unsafe and unreadable outcomes, and fallback review internal first.
lane_matrix.actual_lane_summary: Use a managed scanner later only when quarantine and audit semantics remain stronger than product convenience.
lane_matrix.seq035_handoff_focus: protection-plan or API tenant creation, eventing, region choice, retention, and quarantine policy mapping

lane_matrix.mock_lane_summary: The internal combined mock exists only to prove end-to-end readiness and quarantine law before later provisioning.
lane_matrix.actual_lane_summary: No combined suite is shortlisted by default; transcript and scan providers remain separable.
lane_matrix.seq035_handoff_focus: keep seq_035 family provisioning split even if one cloud vendor supplies multiple primitives

selected_access_rows.exposure_constraints: No markdown literals, no console echo, no trace/video capture, screenshots only after explicit redaction, runtime reference handles only.
selected_access_rows.notes: Mock scanning project principal for quarantine workflow rehearsal.

selected_access_rows.exposure_constraints: Synthetic-only material; never mix with live partner outputs or live patient-like identifiers.
selected_access_rows.notes: Synthetic malware-signature corpus and known-bad evidence pack.

selected_access_rows.exposure_constraints: No markdown literals, no console echo, no trace/video capture, screenshots only after explicit redaction, runtime reference handles only.

selected_access_rows.risk_refs: HZ_TELEPHONY_EVIDENCE_INADEQUACY; HZ_URGENT_DIVERSION_UNDER_OR_OVER_TRIAGE; RISK_EXT_COMMS_VENDOR_DELAY; RISK_MUTATION_003; RISK_STATE_004; RISK_MUTATION_001
selected_access_rows.source_refs: blueprint/phase-2-the-identity-and-echoes.md#2F. Caller verification, voice capture, transcript stub, and SMS continuation; data/analysis/external_dependencies.json#dep_transcription_processing_provider

transcription.retention_and_deletion_notes: Transcript deletion removes uploaded files; region selection and private deployment reduce retention ambiguity.
transcription.notes: AssemblyAI is strong on job callbacks, EU region selection, and deletion semantics. Like Deepgram, callback trust still needs Vecells-side replay fencing and trusted re-fetch.
transcription.portability_notes: Private deployment and explicit delete APIs keep exit posture workable.

transcription.retention_and_deletion_notes: Hosted service can callback results; private and self-hosted deployment options reduce retention lock-in.
transcription.notes: Focused transcription provider with strong async and streaming support. Callback authenticity remains a bounded weakness, so Vecells must treat callbacks as hints and re-fetch job state before promotion.
transcription.pricing_notes: Usage-priced with explicit developer and enterprise paths.
transcription.portability_notes: Private deployment options reduce lock-in risk relative to cloud-only providers.

artifact_scanning.retention_and_deletion_notes: Results stay in AWS control planes and S3 tags/events instead of direct file round-trips through a vendor callback endpoint.
artifact_scanning.notes: Best fit for quarantine-first artifact handling because results are event-driven, taggable, and explicit about failure or unsupported states. The tradeoff is AWS account coupling.
artifact_scanning.project_model: AWS account plus GuardDuty protection plan, IAM role, and EventBridge
artifact_scanning.sandbox_posture: cloud account and S3 prefix-scoped plans
artifact_scanning.pricing_notes: Per-object and per-GB pricing; strong need for spend boundaries.
artifact_scanning.portability_notes: Good event semantics, but higher account and storage coupling than API-key scanners.

artifact_scanning.retention_and_deletion_notes: Cloud-service regions and explicit API posture are documented; file handling remains more controllable than threat-intel-first services.
artifact_scanning.notes: Strong multi-engine scan vendor with explicit API docs and region story. It is weaker than GuardDuty on event-driven posture but stronger than lighter-weight API scanners for quarantine evidence.
artifact_scanning.pricing_notes: Commercial managed service; cost governance must be explicit.
artifact_scanning.portability_notes: API posture is clear, but multi-engine semantics still need adapter-bound normalization.

transcription.retention_and_deletion_notes: Batch output is tightly coupled to S3 input and output configuration, which is good for audit but broadens the infrastructure surface too early.
transcription.notes: Amazon Transcribe has strong partial-result semantics, but the lack of first-class callback delivery and the S3/KMS coupling make it a weaker seq_035 handoff than the shortlist pair.
transcription.project_model: AWS account, IAM, S3 input/output buckets
transcription.sandbox_posture: cloud account and bucket policy driven
transcription.pricing_notes: Usage-based AWS billing with broader account guardrails needed.
transcription.portability_notes: Output-storage coupling increases exit cost relative to focused API providers.

transcription.retention_and_deletion_notes: Batch jobs integrate with customer storage and TTL; data privacy posture is stronger than many focused API vendors but the environment model is heavier.
transcription.notes: Azure is technically credible and safer on storage and privacy than a toy API, but seq_035 would inherit storage, networking, and tenant setup before the product-side adapter is ready.
transcription.project_model: Azure resource plus storage account and webhook registration
transcription.pricing_notes: Cloud subscription pricing; more infrastructure coupling than focused vendors.
transcription.portability_notes: Containers help, but the account and storage model still couples provider choice with broader platform choices.

artifact_scanning.retention_and_deletion_notes: Deployment options are flexible, but the API posture is more scan-request oriented than quarantine-workflow oriented.
artifact_scanning.notes: Useful portable API-key scanner, but it scores below the shortlist because it is lighter on explicit asynchronous evidence states and webhook-friendly eventing.
artifact_scanning.pricing_notes: Hosted or dedicated pricing paths exist.
artifact_scanning.portability_notes: Deployment flexibility is good, but audit and state semantics are thinner than the shortlist.

transcription.retention_and_deletion_notes: Regional endpoints and batch APIs exist, but the official product page warning about sensitive or personal data is not acceptable for this use case without much stronger contractual evidence.
transcription.kill_switch_reason_if_any: Official product posture warns against entering sensitive, confidential, or personal information; that is incompatible with this evidence-processing seam until clarified with stronger contract evidence.
transcription.notes: This is not a product-capability rejection. It is a current-official-docs rejection for this healthcare evidence-processing seam.
transcription.project_model: Google Cloud project and recognizer resources
transcription.pricing_notes: Published pricing exists, but the current product warning dominates.
transcription.portability_notes: Standard cloud portability issues plus unresolved policy fit.

artifact_scanning.retention_and_deletion_notes: Private scanning is isolated from the public corpus, but the product remains oriented to threat-intel workflows rather than quarantine-first evidence promotion.
artifact_scanning.kill_switch_reason_if_any: Threat-intel-oriented scanning and weaker quarantine workflow fit make it a poor match for governed patient-evidence intake.
artifact_scanning.notes: Rejected because the operational model fits analyst triage better than patient-evidence quarantine and bounded review.
artifact_scanning.pricing_notes: Commercial engagement required; no advantage over better shortlist options here.
artifact_scanning.portability_notes: Adapter fit is weaker than GuardDuty or OPSWAT for this seam.

combined.retention_and_deletion_notes: AWS can cover both families, but the combined stack would force shared storage, IAM, and event-plane choices too early.
combined.kill_switch_reason_if_any: A single AWS stack would over-couple transcript and scan failure domains, storage posture, and onboarding friction before the adapter contract is frozen.
combined.notes: Rejected as the default suite winner. Individual AWS services remain viable where their family-specific fit is strong.
combined.project_model: AWS account and shared control plane
combined.sandbox_posture: cloud account and shared event/storage model
combined.pricing_notes: Billing and operational overhead spread across multiple AWS services.
combined.portability_notes: Suite coupling is the main reason for rejection, not the raw technical capability.

transcription.retention_and_deletion_notes: Synthetic fixtures only. Deterministic resets and explicit supersession keep replay, deletion, and audit behavior local to the repo.
transcription.notes: Canonical local transcription twin. It must preserve readiness, coverage class, quality band, and supersession semantics before any real provider is admissible.
transcription.project_model: repository-local simulator service plus deterministic credential seed
transcription.portability_notes: This is the baseline contract the real-later shortlist must match.

artifact_scanning.retention_and_deletion_notes: Synthetic-only signature packs, explicit quarantine manifests, and deterministic resets keep unsafe evidence local and replayable.
artifact_scanning.notes: Canonical local artifact-scan twin. It must preserve clean, suspicious, quarantined, unreadable, and failed outcomes without silently passing evidence onward.
artifact_scanning.project_model: repository-local simulator service plus signature fixture dataset
artifact_scanning.portability_notes: This twin is the acceptance contract for any later managed scanner.

combined.retention_and_deletion_notes: Repository-local only. All transcript and scan events remain synthetic, replayable, and explicit.
combined.notes: Combined internal mock lane only. It exists to prove readiness, quarantine, and fallback semantics before any live vendor is trusted.
combined.portability_notes: The actual-later strategy must adapt to this internal contract, not replace it.

decision_log.title: Split providers remain the default actual-later posture
decision_log.summary: Transcription and artifact scanning are kept independently selectable to prevent one vendor stack from becoming silent product truth.

decision_log.title: The internal mock lane stays authoritative for simulator fidelity
decision_log.summary: The transcript and scan twins are the development baseline, not disposable placeholders.

decision_log.title: Callback deliveries are hints, not proof
decision_log.summary: Even shortlisted providers may only advance readiness through Vecells-side job-state re-fetch and governed evidence assimilation.

decision_log.title: Google Cloud Speech-to-Text is rejected on current official posture
decision_log.summary: The current official product warning about sensitive or personal information is too strong to ignore for this seam.

decision_log.title: Threat-intel-first scanning is not enough for patient evidence quarantine
decision_log.summary: VirusTotal Private Scanning is rejected because quarantine workflow fit is weaker than the shortlist alternatives.

decision_log.title: Local or self-hosted scanning still remains preferable in some deployments
decision_log.summary: If binary evidence cannot leave the platform trust boundary or a regulator demands deterministic quarantine control, keep scanning local and treat managed vendors as optional later augmentation.
